hypothesis: A direction-neutral, predeclared OCO breakout bracket around exogenously scheduled US macro releases can capture discontinuous FX futures displacement without predicting direction and can deliver materially greater target velocity than the falsified cross-sectional residual grammar.
strongest_argument_against: One-minute OHLCV cannot order both bracket touches inside one bar and conservative ambiguity handling plus stop-market costs may eliminate the apparent release edge.
smallest_decisive_experiment: Freeze official release times and a finite OCO lattice, screen on 2023 discovery, validate on late-2023, and open 2024 pre-Q4 FX outcomes once only for policies positive under stressed costs in both earlier roles.

causal_contract.direction_selection: NONE; both sides of one OCO bracket are frozen before the scheduled release
causal_contract.feature_cutoff: only bars with timestamp strictly before release_time may set bracket levels
causal_contract.order_activation: bracket becomes active at the frozen scheduled release timestamp
causal_contract.entry: first observed one-minute bar touching exactly one side; gap fills use the worse of trigger and bar open
causal_contract.dual_touch: conservative immediate full-stop loss; never discard an ambiguous volatile event
causal_contract.exit: first causal stop, target, time-stop, roll guard or mandatory session flatten
causal_contract.continuous_roll_guard: lookback and position must retain one explicit instrument_id

decision_policy.signal: At least one frozen finalist has positive stressed net in validation and final-development, positive paired stressed uplift over its prior-day same-clock control, no MLL breach, and nonzero account target progress.
decision_policy.weak: Gross release displacement is positive but no finalist survives stressed execution and matched timing control.
decision_policy.falsified: No policy is positive under stressed costs in both discovery and validation, or every opened finalist fails final-development/control economics.
